hook: Bananas are berries. Strawberries aren't. Everything backwards.

scenes.line: Scientists have a totally different definition of berry than you do.
scenes.visual_prompt: A split-screen comparison: on the left, a ripe yellow banana; on the right, a bright red strawberry. Both glowing softly against a neutral dark background, with a thin white line dividing them.

scenes.line: A true berry forms from a single flower's ovary after pollination.
scenes.visual_prompt: Cross-section diagram of a banana flower's interior, with glowing yellow lines tracing from ovary to developing fruit, scientific botanical illustration style.

scenes.line: The ovary grows thick walls, fills with seeds, and boom—you have a berry.
scenes.visual_prompt: Animated macro close-up of banana flesh developing, tiny seeds embedded throughout, soft yellow-cream colors swirling and solidifying frame by frame.

scenes.line: Bananas check all three boxes. They're 100 percent, botanically, berries.
scenes.visual_prompt: A glowing checkmark appearing next to a banana in a clean, minimal interface style, bright green against soft dark background.

scenes.line: Strawberries? They develop from the flower's base, not the ovary.
scenes.visual_prompt: Cutaway cross-section of a strawberry showing its internal structure, with red rays highlighting the seeds on the exterior surface against white flesh.

scenes.line: Those tiny seeds you see are the actual fruits. The red part is just wrapper.
scenes.visual_prompt: Extreme close-up macro shot of strawberry surface, individual seed details visible, dramatically lit to emphasize the texture and structure.

scenes.line: So strawberries are technically aggregate accessory fruits. Basically an impostor.
scenes.visual_prompt: A strawberry rotates slowly in 3D space, its surface marked with dotted lines highlighting each seed as a separate component, modern geometric style.

scenes.line: Your whole fruit hierarchy just got botanically rearranged.
scenes.visual_prompt: A stylized fruit taxonomy chart morphing and reorganizing itself, with bananas rising to the top and strawberries shifting sideways, smooth animated transitions.

cta: If plant biology counts, what other 'fruits' do you think aren't actually berries?
